0.response: {"Q0":"When viewing the Galaxy monetary amount, I would repeat to myself \"top\" or \"bottom\" so when it came to selecting the galaxy after the planet stage, I would remember which Galaxy to focus on so I could press the button quickly enough. "}

7.response: {"Q0":"I would look at the probabilities and how different the numbers were. If one number had a very high percentage compared to the other, I would always try to select the higher percentage one even if it gave less money. However, if they were relatively close in percentage I would always pick the higher monetary value. The exemption to this rule was if the difference was very high, and the high number had a probability greater than around 40% I would generally pick the high number. "}

9.response: {"Q0":"I think I didn't realize when the trial ended and the real tasks started. "}

5.response: {"Q0":"I sometimes dug once or twice but caught myself. It was an accident as I felt conditioned to have to dig in all colored planets. "}

2.response: {"Q0":"Probably about 40% of the time there would be gems it felt like."}

8.response: {"Q0":"I think I really focused hard on visiting planets because this aspect was the most in my control, I tried emphasizing not losing gold by accidentally mining on the yellow planet or a planet the astronaut left. I found myself losing concentration a few times during selecting galaxies. "}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes it was very clear as it would specify which gems would not be sellable after."}
